description: DAG execution schedule for Cursor Orchestrator. Agent ids MUST match config/agents.json exactly. No new agents; do not rename. Parallel waves list agents that may run concurrently when all their dependsOn (from agents.json) are satisfied.

rules.parallelism: Within a wave marked parallel=true, run only agents whose dependencies are all complete; agents in the same wave must not depend on each other.
rules.jira: Use Atlassian MCP for Planner-issued issues; per-agent execution should be traceable (issue keys, comments, assignees from config/jira_agent_assignees.json).
rules.git: Meaningful work: feature branch, commit, push, PR, review, merge — as applicable to the repo workflow.

waves.notes: STEP 1 — receive user request, emit work order with correlation_id
STEP 2 — architecture, module breakdown, Jira story structure (MCP); depends on orchestrator
Wave 1 of DAG expansion — controls contracts/policy before engineering
Requires planner + data_governance; unlocks quality, security, tests
All depend on data_engineer; security and data_quality also require data_governance. Run in parallel once deps satisfied.
Requires tester + security + data_engineer per agents.json
cost_optimizer needs data_engineer+devops; data_analyst needs data_quality+planner. Parallel when both are eligible.
Final sink — run after all in-scope upstream agents for this work order have completed (including devops, cost_optimizer, data_analyst when applicable). Consolidate docs/runbooks.
